summary: Matched the ftCommon DownAttack slice by replacing ft_08A4 assembly with a new C implementation and header for `ftCo_DownAttack`. The matched report shows 100% for the file and all listed functions, including `ftCo_800984D4`, `ftCo_8009856C`, `ftCo_DownAttack_Anim`, `ftCo_DownAttack_Phys`, `ftCo_DownAttack_Coll`, `ft_80098634`, `ftCo_800986B0`, and `ftCo_8009872C`. The PR also performed broad cleanup around the global GObj entity list, renaming `HSD_GObj_804D782C` to `HSD_GObj_Entities` and giving the fighter/item list fields typed names.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DownAttack.c` with C implementations for the DownAttack transition, animation, IASA, physics, collision, fighter-list copy helper, and down-attack condition helpers.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DownAttack.h` and moved the relevant prototypes out of the broader `ft_08A4.h` where appropriate.
Removed the matched DownAttack assembly from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`; the file-level matching report shows `0/19100` score and 100.00%.
Added the new C object to `obj_files.mk` immediately before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o` object.
Renamed the global GObj list symbol from `HSD_GObj_804D782C` to `HSD_GObj_Entities` across many asm and C references.
Renamed `HSD_GObjList` fields from `x20_fighters` and `x24_items` to typed `fighters` and `items`, using `Fighter_GObj*` and `Item_GObj*` in `gobj.h`.
Updated existing fighter and item loops to use `HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters` and `HSD_GObj_Entities->items` instead of raw offset-style names.
Expanded `HSD_ObjAllocUnk2` in `src/melee/it/types.h` with named float/int/Vec3 fields through `x150`, supporting the matched `ft_80098634` layout.
Moved the `{ 14, 0, -3, 3 }` Vec4 constant used by `ft_80098634` into the DownAttack C file as a local static `unk_vec4`, and removed `ftDynamics_803C5710` from `ftdynamics.c`.
Added `CONV_LO` and `CONV_HI` constants to `placeholder.h`; the exact downstream matching role is not explicit in the excerpt.
smart_moves: Used a small `static inline bool inlineA0(ftCo_Fighter* fp)` to preserve the assembly shape for the A/B input check while keeping the high-level code readable.
Kept the empty IASA callback as `void ftCo_DownAttack_IASA(ftCo_GObj* gobj) {}` rather than forcing unnecessary logic, matching the original `blr` behavior.
For motion-state selection, expressed the up/down DownAttack choice with a ternary against `ftCo_MS_DownWaitU`, mapping to `ftCo_MS_DownAttackU` or `ftCo_MS_DownAttackD`.
Matched `ft_80098634` by treating the passed allocation object through multiple typed views (`Vec4*` and `Vec3*`) to reproduce the original strided writes.
Improved global list readability at the same time as matching by introducing `HSD_GObj_Entities`, `fighters`, and `items`, reducing future reliance on magic offsets `0x20` and `0x24`.
Preserved a known matching artifact in `ftCo_8009872C` with a guarded `MUST_MATCH` unused stack array instead of contaminating normal builds.
decomp_lessons: When matching small fighter state files, split the completed functions into a dedicated `ftCo_*` C file and leave unrelated unmatched assembly in the original asm object.
A precise GObj-list type pays off across subsystems: replacing `x20_fighters` and `x24_items` with `fighters` and `items` makes loops self-documenting and helps later decompilation.

Object-allocation structs can be partially named only as far as needed for a match; `HSD_ObjAllocUnk2` was expanded around the offsets used by `ft_80098634` without requiring a full reverse-engineered type.
For callbacks that simply delegate, high-level wrappers such as `ftCo_DownAttack_Phys -> ft_80084F3C` and `ftCo_DownAttack_Coll -> ft_80084104` can match cleanly and document action-state behavior.
If a global constant was previously parked in an unrelated translation unit only because assembly referenced it, move it next to the matched C code once its local role is known.
Retain evidence of matching hacks, such as `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused stack padding, so future cleanup can distinguish required codegen artifacts from intended gameplay logic.
naming_conventions: `HSD_GObj_804D782C` was renamed to the semantic global `HSD_GObj_Entities`.
`HSD_GObjList::x20_fighters` was renamed to `fighters` and typed as `Fighter_GObj*`.
`HSD_GObjList::x24_items` was renamed to `items` and typed as `Item_GObj*`.
DownAttack callbacks follow the common action callback naming pattern: `ftCo_DownAttack_Anim`, `ftCo_DownAttack_IASA`, `ftCo_DownAttack_Phys`, and `ftCo_DownAttack_Coll`.
Unknown address-based helpers remained address named where behavior was not fully named, such as `ftCo_800984D4`, `ftCo_800986B0`, `ftCo_8009872C`, and `ft_80098634`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a contiguous set of functions in `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` with C while leaving remaining assembly in place, using object ordering in `obj_files.mk` to preserve layout.
Used `Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, msid, Ft_MF_None, NULL, 0, 1, 0)` followed by `ftAnim_8006EBA4(gobj)` to match `ftCo_8009856C`.
Used `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining` with a negated check and fallback to `ft_8008A2BC` to match the DownAttack animation-end transition.
Used a guarded unused stack block in `ftCo_8009872C` under `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to satisfy stack/codegen requirements.
Matched fighter-list traversal through `HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters` and `cur_gobj = cur_gobj->next` rather than raw `lwz` offsets.
The new `HSD_ObjAllocUnk2` layout let the C code write floats at the front, copy fighter position data into later Vec3 slots, and store a count at `x150`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document `ftCo_800984D4`, `ftCo_800986B0`, `ftCo_8009872C`, or `ft_80098634`.
Search for subsequent cleanup of `HSD_ObjAllocUnk2` and whether its fields get a real domain-specific type.
Check later PRs for further migration from `HSD_GObj_804D782C` to `HSD_GObj_Entities` in remaining assembly.
Investigate whether `CONV_LO` and `CONV_HI` added to `placeholder.h` were later replaced by a shared integer-to-double conversion convention.
Review later `ftCommon` Down/DownStand/DownBound PRs for reuse of the DownAttack state callback and motion-state patterns.
